claim_text: We present a nonadiabatic classical-trajectory approach that offers the best of both worlds between fewest-switches surface hopping (FSSH) and quasiclassical mapping dynamics.

claim_text: This mapping approach to surface hopping (MASH) propagates the nuclei on the active adiabatic potential-energy surface, like in FSSH.

claim_text: This guarantees internal consistency between the active surface and the electronic degrees of freedom throughout the dynamics.

claim_text: This jump procedure is able to describe quantum interference phenomena, which cannot be included by standard FSSH decoherence schemes.
